topic_title: Every API call is a design decision — latency, cost, and reliability considered upfront

format_discipline.h1_text: Every API Call Is a Design Decision — Latency, Cost, and Reliability Considered Upfront
format_discipline.detail: No frontmatter block. First non-empty line is the # H1. reading.meta.json parses cleanly. Exactly 6 required ## sections present in correct order.

length_budget.detail: 980 words is within 800–1200 target. 980/230 = 4.26 min read-aloud time, within 4–6 min target.

citation_discipline.detail: Both resources cited throughout. [1] Artificial Analysis maps to artificialanalysis.ai (primary) — cited in Key Concepts, Worked Example, In Practice, Key Takeaways. [2] Finout maps to finout.io (supporting) — cited in Key Concepts cost table, In Practice, Key Takeaways. No orphan markers or orphan bibliography entries.

forward_references.detail: message.usage attributed to topic 12.11. try/except, retry logic, time.sleep() attributed to topic 12.13. Latency, cost formula, model selection, three-question checklist are core topic-14 content. Batch API and prompt caching (corpus mention-and-defer items) are absent from the reading — correctly omitted. No references to 13.x or 14.x material.

sample_results.assertion: Haiku returns in under one second; Opus can take fifteen seconds or more
One token is roughly four characters of English
$2.50/day on Haiku vs $150/day on Opus — sixty times the bill
Rate limits are per-account caps on requests and tokens per minute
Haiku ~$0.25/$1.25, Sonnet ~$3/$15, Opus ~$15/$75 per million tokens
Start on Haiku, step up to Sonnet if quality fails, reserve Opus for proven Sonnet insufficiency
Three-question checklist (need call? how many? which tier?)
call_and_measure function uses time.time() for latency and message.usage for token counts
In Practice bullets: test cheapest first, count calls, set max_tokens deliberately, model in a variable, log usage
Defaulting to Opus 'just to be safe' is the most common beginner mistake

faithfulness.detail: All 10 sampled assertions trace directly to the corpus. Zero fabricated claims detected.

beginner_readability.detail: Latency, tokens, rate limits, queue time all defined on first use with plain-language meanings inline. No unexplained acronyms (RPM/TPM are not used; plain-language phrasing is substituted). No paragraph introduces 3+ new terms simultaneously. Concepts lead with concrete examples or analogies (latency measured in seconds, cost shown as dollar amounts, model tiers introduced with specific use-case bullets). Sentences are short and active-voice throughout. Zero hedging phrases detected. Total soft findings: 0 — well below the ≥3 escalation threshold.

formatting.detail: No paragraph exceeds 4 sentences. Worked Example step-by-step is a numbered list (5 items). In Practice section is a bulleted list. The three design questions are a numbered list. Model tier comparisons use bold-term/plain-meaning shape. Pricing data is in a table. Inline examples appear in Key Concepts alongside concept introductions — cost formula is shown with the pricing table immediately; latency is shown with seconds figures immediately. Formatting standards from artifact-mix.json are met.

assessment_references.detail: No assessment ID (A4), due-week number, or grade weight appears in the reading artifact. The cost-and-latency note is framed as a 'standard professional deliverable' without LMS metadata.

scope.detail: All content is within the headline: latency, cost (token pricing, model tiers), reliability (framed as a design question, mechanics deferred to 12.13), model selection, the three design questions, and a worked measuring example. Batch API and prompt caching from the corpus's mention-and-defer are absent from the reading. No prompt engineering, RAG, or agent concepts appear. Scope is clean.

gold_example_comparison.assessment: Reading meets the gold standard. Structure mirrors the 6-section template used in gold examples. Tone is consistently plain and second-person. Formatting density (bold definitions, inline tables, numbered worked example steps) matches the gold examples. Beginner entry level is consistent with 3-4-how-llms-work (the closest comparator in domain complexity).

summary: PASS — all 8 checks passed with zero findings. Format is clean (no frontmatter, correct H1, valid sidecar, 6 sections in order). Word count 980 is within 800–1200 target; read-aloud time 4.26 min is within 4–6 min target. Both resources (primary [1] artificialanalysis.ai, supporting [2] finout.io) are cited throughout with no orphan markers or entries. No forward references — 12.11 and 12.13 concepts are correctly attributed; batch API/prompt caching correctly omitted. Faithfulness: 10 of 10 sampled assertions trace to the corpus, zero fabricated claims. Beginner readability: all terms defined on first use, no jargon dumps, concrete-first throughout, zero hedging. Formatting: no walls of text, procedures as numbered lists, bold-term/plain-meaning definitions, inline examples in every conceptual section. No assessment references. No scope creep. Reading is ready for HITL #3.
